  "workable": [],
  "findings": [
    "PVC flue pipe is back-pitched, preventing proper drainage of acidic condensate back to the furnace trap.",
    "Potential for condensate pooling, which can block the flue vent, cause pressure switch faults, or leak into unapproved areas."
  ],
  "actions": [
    "Re-slope the PVC flue piping to maintain a minimum upward pitch of 1/4 inch per foot toward the termination or back to the appliance as specified by the manufacturer.",
    "Ensure proper support hangers are installed at required intervals to prevent future sagging or back-pitching."
  ],
  "rationale": "Condensing furnace vent piping must slope back to the appliance to allow condensate to drain properly. A back-pitched pipe will trap water, leading to system shutdown or hazardous flue gas spillage if the vent becomes completely blocked.",
  "references": [
    "IRC M1801",
    "NFPA 54",
    "Manufacturer Installation Instructions"
  ]
}
